description.purpose: treatment_tradeoff — 30-year-old female with my the wheezing gets worse sometimes for a few weeks
description.clinical_scenario: Patient presents with my the wheezing gets worse sometimes for a few weeks. Requires history taking, examination, diagnosis, and treatment planning.
description.focus: Weighing treatment options with comorbidity considerations

user_scenario.persona: 30-year-old female patient, 小学 education, works as a cleaner. a patient who may forget to mention some symptoms presenting with my the wheezing gets worse sometimes. Accompanied by son (age 18).

patient_knows.general_concern: my the wheezing gets worse sometimes

economic_concerns: How much will this medication cost per month?
Can we only prescribe what's absolutely necessary?

share_only_if_asked: acid reflux (needs doctor to specifically ask)
information_sharing_strategy.hidden_note: Only revealed through careful, specific questioning by the doctor
information_sharing_strategy.misleading_note: Symptoms that may point toward an incorrect diagnosis or overlap with other conditions. Doctor must carefully differentiate through targeted questioning and testing.

progressive_reveal.trigger: doctor asks a specific follow-up question about related symptoms

family_member_will_interject: son: How can we help at home?
son: What should we cook for them?

user_scenario.task_instructions: You are HuangXX, a 30-year-old female patient. Your education level is 小学. You work as a cleaner. You are seeing a doctor because of asthma. This has been going on for about a few weeks. Answer honestly but you may forget to mention some symptoms. If the doctor doesn't specifically ask about something, you might not remember it. Your memory for details is not great — approximate answers are fine. You have some symptoms you haven't mentioned to anyone — only reveal them if the doctor specifically asks about them. You are sensitive about medical costs. If the doctor suggests expensive tests or medications, express concern about affordability. Your son is with you and may speak up during the consultation to ask questions or express concerns.

family_member_scenario.scenario: son accompanies patient, participates in the entire visit
family_member_scenario.role: concerned and supportive, may express economic worries
interaction_timing: When medication is discussed, the family member asks about costs
After diagnosis is explained, the family member asks about heritability
When a prescription is written, the family member asks about side effects
When lifestyle changes are mentioned, the family member asks for specifics

communication_checks.criteria: Patient should understand the diagnosis and treatment plan
Uses clear, patient-friendly language appropriate to education level
Addresses patient concerns, misconceptions, and questions

chief_complaint_detail.description: Complete chief complaint with onset, duration, severity, and aggravating/relieving factors

symptoms_completeness.description: All major symptoms of asthma identified through systematic questioning

past_medical_history.description: Relevant past medical history including comorbidities

current_medications.description: Current medication list with doses and adherence

must_ask: Have you been exposed to any new allergens or irritants?
Does the wheezing get worse at night or early morning?
How often are you using your rescue inhaler?

drug_interaction_check.description: Must check drug interactions before prescribing

forbidden_actions: Agree with patient misconceptions without correction
Ignore abnormal lab values without explanation or follow-up plan
Diagnose without asking about symptom onset, duration, and progression
Diagnose without exploring at least one differential diagnosis
Dismiss patient concerns without addressing them
Use medical jargon without explanation when patient has limited health literacy
Fail to correct patient misconceptions about treatment
Prescribe contraindicated drug without documenting rationale
Order unnecessary tests without clinical justification
Prescribe beta-blockers without considering pulmonary contraindication
Fail to consider COPD as alternative diagnosis
Diagnose asthma without ordering any supporting lab tests

uncertainty_events.event: Lab results show inconsistent or borderline findings
uncertainty_events.example: normal chest X-ray with significant dyspnea
uncertainty_events.expected_handling: Order repeat/confirmatory test, consider pre-analytical error, correlate with clinical symptoms before concluding

uncertainty_events.event: Patient casually mentions 'acid reflux' when discussing treatment options
uncertainty_events.example: 'Oh, I also sometimes get acid reflux — is that related?'
uncertainty_events.expected_handling: Recognize this may indicate undiagnosed acid reflux, update differential, order appropriate workup
